summary: Successfully implemented native Google Sign-In for Android using Google Play Services, eliminating web OAuth redirects to localhost. Created GoogleSignInPlugin.java native Android plugin, googleSignInNative.ts TypeScript bridge, and updated firebase.ts to handle credential exchange. All components compiled and committed successfully.

files_modified.description: Native Android Capacitor plugin for Google Sign-In using Google Play Services
TypeScript bridge for native Google Sign-In plugin

files_modified.reason: Google Play Services library required for native Google Sign-In

lines: 165-175: Platform detection for Android native flow
changes: Platform detection in signInWithOAuth(): if (platform === 'android') → use native
New function for ID token exchange

files_modified.reason: Already uses supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth() which now routes to native on Android

runtime_logs_to_verify: [Login] Using NATIVE GOOGLE SIGN-IN for Android
[Firebase Auth] Native plugin returned token for: <email>
expected_behavior: System account chooser appears (Google Material Design UI)
No localhost in OAuth flow logs
User navigates to CharacterSelect after sign-in

architecture.flow: User clicks Google button → Platform detection (Android) → Native plugin opened → System account chooser → ID token returned → Firebase credential exchange → User authenticated → App navigates to CharacterSelect
architecture.key_innovation: Eliminates web-based OAuth redirect to localhost by using native Google Play Services account picker

git_commit.message: feat(android-auth): implement native Google Sign-In using Google Play Services

confidence_notes: Not tested on actual Android device yet, but all compilation, architecture, and error handling are correct. Runtime behavior verification needed.
fallback_chain: Secondary: Web popup flow (existing, still available)
prerequisites: Firebase project must have Google provider enabled
Android device must have Google Play Services installed

pre_test: Verify device has Google Play Services
runtime: Verify logs contain all validation signals
Restart app and verify session persists

next_steps: Verify all log signals appear during sign-in
Monitor Firebase Authentication logs in console
